qa_pairs.question: What is the primary function of high-end I/O libraries in relation to data structures?
qa_pairs.answer: High-end I/O libraries map application abstractions to storage abstractions I/O in terms of the data structures of the code.

qa_pairs.question: How do high-end I/O libraries store data?
qa_pairs.answer: Data is stored directly to the disk by calling these libraries.

qa_pairs.question: Are high-end I/O libraries designed for efficiency?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, high-end I/O libraries are implemented to work quite efficiently.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it recommended to use high-end I/O libraries like HDF5 and NetCDF?
qa_pairs.answer: It is better to use these kinds of libraries because both HDF5 and NetCDF are supported.

qa_pairs.question: What I/O middleware can users utilize in addition to high-end libraries?
qa_pairs.answer: Users could also use I/O middleware, which is MPI-IO.

qa_pairs.question: What will be the main focus of the "today's talk" regarding I/O strategies?
qa_pairs.answer: The talk will focus more on MPI-IO, which is a part of MPI-2.

qa_pairs.question: What does the 'application' refer to in the context of choosing I/O methods?
qa_pairs.answer: The application mostly refers to the user's program.

qa_pairs.question: Who or what ultimately decides whether to use a high-end I/O library or I/O middleware?
qa_pairs.answer: The application, which is mostly the user's program, will decide whether to use a high-end I/O library or I/O middleware.
